page: UpCloud 4xCPU-8GB is a general-purpose virtual server hosted on the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an AMD EPYC 7542 processor running at 2.0 GHz. The configuration includes 4 vCPUs allocated on a shared basis, with 4 physical cores and 1 thread per core. The system is equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding 2.0 GB of RAM per core, and includes 160 GB of local SSD storage. Network connectivity includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Performance benchmarks indicate a profile suited for moderate workloads. The AMD EPYC processor delivers average single-core performance, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1270.0 and a Passmark CPU score of 7831.67. However, multi-core performance is relatively weak, as evidenced by a stress-ng multi-core score of 3099.37 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 3979.0. Memory performance is mixed; small-block read-only cached bandwidth is average at 387.96 GB/sec, while large-block uncached read bandwidth is weak at 33.25 GB/sec.

Under database and web serving tests, the server displays moderate capabilities. Passmark database operations score in the average tier at 2645.0, though Redis SET operations are weak at 5.22 million operations per second. Static web serving throughput is also in the lower tier at 4.21 GB/sec. For machine learning tasks, the server handles small 135M LLM models with average efficiency, achieving 157.04 tokens per second for text generation, but performance drops significantly on larger 7B models.

This hardware configuration offers a cost-efficient entry point for resource-constrained environments that do not require dedicated CPU threads. The shared CPU allocation and 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio make it suitable for lightweight web serving, basic database operations, software compilation, and general-purpose utility workloads.
description: UpCloud 4xCPU-8GB is an x86_64 general-purpose virtual server running on the KVM hypervisor. It is configured with an AMD EPYC 7542 processor operating at 2.0 GHz, providing 4 shared vCPUs across 4 physical cores. The instance includes 8.0 GB of RAM, 160 GB of local SSD storage, and one public IPv4 address. Benchmark data reveals average single-core CPU performance and cached memory bandwidth, though multi-core execution and uncached memory speeds are relatively weak. Database operations and small-model LLM inference perform at average levels, while Redis throughput and static web serving remain low. Given its shared CPU allocation and moderate resource density, this server is best suited for budget-conscious, non-intensive workloads such as lightweight web hosting, basic database tasks, and general-purpose application development.
og_description: UpCloud 4xCPU-8GB is a general-purpose virtual server featuring 4 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 8.0 GB of RAM, and 160 GB of local SSD storage, delivering moderate performance for basic workloads.

tagline: A budget-friendly virtual server combining shared AMD EPYC processing, local SSD storage, and balanced memory for lightweight utility workloads.

160 GB of local SSD storage and 1 complimentary public IPv4 address
Average single-core CPU performance and cached memory bandwidth
Suitable for lightweight web serving, basic database tasks, and development
Cost-efficient shared-core resource profile for budget-conscious deployments
